projects.name: Scrite
projects.description: Scrite is open-source multi-lingual screenwriting app, that lets you write your films better. With support for industry standard formatting, visual mapping of screenplay structures, elaborate research & note-taking features and a comprehensive set of pre-production reports, Scrite is a great place to start writing your next screenplay, or even import & continue working on your existing ones.
projects.short_description: Multilingual Screenwriting App for Mac and PC

form.question: How to support
form.response: Spread the word—invite filmmakers to try the app, share video reviews, contribute guides or code, and join our Discord to help new users.

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: Scrite is a screenwriting software designed for Indian languages, enabling writers to create professionally formatted scripts, visually structure stories, refine narratives with ease, and generate detailed pre-production reports.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: You don't have to respond to everything immediately. Breathe. Relax. Take your time.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: I love watching films, and it’s exciting to know that many of them are now being written using Scrite.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
form.response: Not sure if this can be a theme song, but this is one song that got me through a lot of coding in Scrite. I have no idea why. Its my go-to song to unlock the mood to build long and elaborate stuff in the app: https://music.youtube.com/watch?v=jt96zlTUyLQ

form.question: If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
